description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a series of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its top surface, creating a striped pattern. The grooves are deep and dark, contrasting with the mottled, earthy tones of the stone, which range from brown to gray and black. The block has a solid, robust appearance, with sharp, clean edges and a uniform thickness. The sides of the block are smooth and unadorned, emphasizing the functional, utilitarian design. The overall finish is matte, with a slightly rough texture typical of cut or cast stone. This type of block is commonly used as an urban obstacle or barrier, such as in plazas, sidewalks, or parks, to control pedestrian or vehicular movement.
description: A rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on top, featuring mottled brown, gray, and black tones and a matte, slightly rough finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the block and the deep, parallel grooves running horizontally across the top surface.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the grooves and the uniform thickness of the block, with grooves running vertically from this perspective.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the same striped groove pattern and solid construction.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the vertical grooves and the block's consistent thickness.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a barrier or seating element
attribute_installation_method: set in concrete or on a prepared gravel base
attribute_weathering_pattern: No significant weathering is visible; the surface appears clean with a consistent matte finish and no chips or erosion.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or spaced in a row as an obstacle or barrier
